name: Rhubarb Bakewell Tart
recipeIngredient: 1/2 piece preserved stem ginger in syrup, very finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 200°C/400°F/Gas 6. Sift the flour into a large bowl and rub in the butter until the mixture resembles fine breadcrumbs. Stir in the caster sugar and enough water to form a soft dough. Roll out on a floured surface, line a 23cm loose-bottomed deep flan tin with it, chill for 20 minutes.
2. Line the lined tin with crumpled greaseproof paper and fill with baking beans. Bake for 10 minutes, remove the paper and beans, then bake for a further 10 minutes.
3. In a pan, combine the rhubarb, ginger, and sugar. Cover and cook over low heat for 4-5 minutes until the rhubarb has softened. Uncover and boil for 2-3 more minutes until the mixture has a jam-like consistency.
4. Cream the butter and caster sugar together until pale. Add the eggs, one at a time, stirring in the almond essence between additions. Sift the flour, salt, and baking powder into the creamed mixture with the ground almonds.
5. Spread the rhubarb mixture over the base of the pastry case. Spoon over the almond mixture and level the surface. Sprinkle with flaked almonds. Bake for 35 minutes until golden brown. Melt the apricot jam and lemon juice together over low heat and brush over the tart. Serve with cream.
